note: Codex ran a read-only correctness + in-frame-style pass (sandbox blocked its writes); all valid findings were then applied manually this run. Verified the answer.md code line-by-line against the canonical verl compute_policy_loss_cispo in core_algos.py (clamp log-ratio to [-20,20], exp, clamp IS weight to [1-low,1+high], DETACH the clipped weight, loss = -clipped_ratio_sg * advantages * log_prob, token-mean agg, pg_clipfrac = masked_mean(ratio != clipped_ratio), no dual-clip floor, pg_clipfrac_lower = 0.0) — semantically faithful, no token dropped by a surrogate gate. Fixes applied: (1) sharpened the stop-gradient math in reasoning.md to state the precise reason detach is needed (without it the product rule adds a spurious A*log_pi*grad(clip(w)) term — zero out-of-band re-creating the gate, nonzero in-band double-counting) rather than the looser 'flat region zeroes the gradient'; (2) softened an unsupported 'reference design leaves the low edge wide' claim in answer.md to 'in practice the high edge carries the trade-off'; (3) rewrote the context.md stop-gradient paragraph so it presents detach as a generic available primitive instead of leaking the final sg[clip(r)] mechanism, keeping the context in-frame (research question + 4-point background + editable interface). Gradient derivation (bounded-coefficient REINFORCE) re-verified correct.
